headline: Malaysian Qualifications Agency Deregisters 12 Programs for Poor Quality
context: MQA revoked accreditation for 12 degree programs at 8 private institutions in 2025 affecting 4,500 enrolled students.

cards.big: 12 degree programs at 8 private institutions lost MQA accreditation in 2025
cards.sub: 4,500 students must transfer or accept qualifications of diminished value

cards.big: MQA inspects only 15% of active programs annually due to staffing constraints
cards.sub: Majority of programs operate for years between quality assessments

cards.big: Affected students were not informed of quality concerns before enrolling in programs
cards.sub: No public early-warning system exists for programs under quality review

cards.big: UK QAA publishes real-time institutional risk ratings accessible to prospective students
cards.sub: Transparency allows informed choice before enrollment not crisis after deregistration

cards.big: Students should never be the last to know their degree program is failing quality standards
Should MQA publish live quality ratings so students can make informed enrollment decisions
